subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand women’s challenges and achievements historically and today
Analyze gender stereotypes and their impact
Develop research, reflection, and communication skills
Encourage critical thinking and civic awareness

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Women’s Challenges
student_activities: List challenges women faced in the past
Share examples of women they know who overcame challenges

lesson_plan.title: Education and Social Change
topics_covered: Examples of women pioneers who studied despite difficulties
teaching_strategy: Show pictures and stories of early women scholars
Class discussion comparing past and present opportunities
student_activities: Write 3 examples of women who changed society through education
Discuss the benefits of equal education

lesson_plan.title: Women Who Made a Difference
topics_covered: Key women reformers and leaders (Ramabai, Rokeya, Rashsundari Devi)
Their struggles, achievements, and societal impact
teaching_strategy: Short videos or slides if available
student_activities: Prepare a short paragraph on one woman leader

lesson_plan.title: Challenging Gender Stereotypes
topics_covered: Common stereotypes about men and women
student_activities: Identify 2–3 stereotypes in school, media, or society
Discuss how these can be changed

lesson_plan.title: Women Today and Equality
topics_covered: Opportunities for women in education, work, and community
teaching_strategy: Class discussion and sharing examples from media or local community
Show images of women in different professions
student_activities: Create a small table comparing women’s roles in past and present

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Reflection Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of key women leaders, education, and equality
Relate lessons to students’ own observations
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures, multiple choice, and short answers
student_activities: Write one reflection about learning from women leaders’ stories

teaching_aids: Pictures of women leaders and reformers
teacher_tip: Encourage research and discussion. Ask students to find examples from their community, media, or books. Make lessons interactive with questions, short projects, and reflections.
